headline: COSA RESOURCES DRILLS 13,900 CPS AT MURPHY LAKE NORTH — Potential Uranium Discovery Lights Up Athabasca Basin
snippet: Cosa Resources (TSXV: COSA) logged up to 13,900 counts per second during drilling at the Murphy Lake North Project in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin, signaling a potential new uranium discovery. The Hurricane Deposit discovery team is advancing follow-up work on what may be a significant radiometric anomaly.

headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,816 — On Track for Fourth Consecutive Weekly Advance as Iran Ceasefire Talks Ease Inflation Fears
snippet: Gold held near $4,816 per ounce on Thursday, extending a four-week run driven by optimism over US-Iran peace negotiations and a weaker US dollar. The metal is up nearly 40% year-over-year, with central bank buying and safe-haven demand continuing to outweigh rate-hike headwinds.

headline: USA RARE EARTH POURS FIRST COMMERCIAL YTTRIUM METAL OUTSIDE CHINA — Strategic Metals Supply Chain Milestone
snippet: USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ: USAR) completed the first commercial pour of 99–99.5% purity yttrium metal at its UK subsidiary Less Common Metals, placing it among the handful of non-Chinese producers of commercial-grade yttrium globally. The milestone advances USAR's fully integrated mine-to-magnet supply chain, with Phase 1a sintered NdFeB magnet production ramping to 600 mtpa by end of Q4 2026.

headline: WEST RED LAKE HITS 215 g/t Au OVER 5.35m AT MADSEN — 904 Complex High-Grade Panel Validates Deep Exploration
snippet: West Red Lake Gold Mines (TSXV: WRLG) reported 215.46 g/t Au over 5.35m at the Austin 904 Complex in the Madsen Mine (Ontario), with companion intercepts of 50.34 g/t over 4.05m and 61.70 g/t over 3m at approximately 650m depth. The 904 Complex is slated as the primary drilling focus for the remainder of 2026, with initial production expected H1 2027.

headline: OMAI GOLD LANDS ON TSXV TOP MOVERS — Increased Indicated Mineral Resources Fuel Guyana Junior Attention
snippet: Omai Gold Mines Corp. (TSXV: OMG) joined the TSX Venture Exchange top market movers list after releasing an increased Indicated Mineral Resource estimate at its flagship Guyana gold project. The resource upgrade adds momentum to the emerging Guyana gold district at a time when the region is drawing major M&A attention.

headline: HARVEST GOLD LOCKS IN 50 DRILL TARGETS — 32km Quebec Corridor Set for 2026 Campaign Near 105 g/t Discovery Hole
snippet: Harvest Gold (TSXV: HVG) finalized 50 drill targets along the 32km Kiask River Mineralized Corridor at its Mosseau/LaBelle property in Quebec's Urban Barry Belt, prioritizing 20 holes for 2026 — eight within 500m of the February discovery hole that returned 105 g/t Au over 1.15m. The corridor has been tested only to 100m depth, leaving substantial room at depth.

headline: THE SILVER BULL IS BACK — Producers and Junior Explorers Drawing Fresh Capital as $100/oz Forecasts Circulate
snippet: Rising silver prices and a structural supply deficit are driving fresh investor interest in silver producers and junior explorers, with analysts projecting $81–$100/oz averages and some outlier calls above $150/oz. Solar sector demand consuming roughly one-fifth of annual global silver supply, plus a weakening dollar and Iran peace optimism, are cited as the key catalysts.

headline: COPPER EYES $6.10/LB TWO-MONTH HIGH — US-Iran Diplomacy Lifts Futures; Chile Plans Permitting Acceleration
snippet: Copper futures climbed toward $6.10 per pound on April 15–16, reaching a two-month high as US-Iran ceasefire diplomacy reduced risk-off sentiment. Chile's new government is simultaneously moving to accelerate copper production by streamlining permitting — a supply-side signal that could cap a price recovery that has the metal up nearly 30% year-over-year.

headline: WARNING: GOLD WEAKENS, OIL SURGES — Analyst Flags Rare Simultaneous Breakdown Signal for Mining Stocks
snippet: Gold dropped 0.29%, silver fell 1.45%, and copper shed 0.61% in the same session while oil pushed higher — a combination analysts flag as a classic setup for deeper mining stock corrections. A strengthening dollar compounding oil-driven inflation pressure is the cited mechanism, with the piece advising caution before adding sector exposure.

headline: STAGFLATION PRINTS, SILVER STILL 'ON SALE' — Hot CPI Shows Metal Lagging Its Own Macro Signal
snippet: A hotter-than-expected CPI print alongside slowing GDP growth has re-ignited stagflation debate, with the author arguing silver remains undervalued as an inflation hedge despite its 150%+ 12-month gain. Measured against money supply growth and the current gold-silver ratio, the piece contends silver is still trading well below historical fair value.
